description: Frontend: Replace HabitsDrawer with real data, add API helpers for habits/review, streak ring SVG, check-in animation, add-habit form
steps: 1. Update frontend/lib/api.ts: add interfaces HabitItem (id, name, frequency, target_days, streak, last_completed, history), WeeklyReview (content: string). Add API functions: fetchHabits(authToken) -> GET /api/habits, checkinHabit(authToken, habitId) -> POST /api/habits/checkin, createHabit(authToken, name, frequency, targetDays) -> POST /api/habits, deleteHabit(authToken, habitId) -> DELETE /api/habits/{id}, fetchWeeklyReview(authToken) -> GET /api/review/weekly.

2. Rewrite frontend/components/drawers/HabitsDrawer.tsx completely: remove hardcoded HABITS array, fetch real habits from API using useEffect+useState. Show each habit with: name, streak (flame icon + number), 7-day completion dots (derive from history array - check which of last 7 days have a completion entry). Add SVG streak ring showing weekly completion rate (completions_this_week / target_days as percentage, circular progress). Add check-in button per habit that calls checkinHabit API. Add framer-motion animation on check-in (scale pulse + color flash). Add inline add-habit form at bottom with name input + frequency selector (daily / 2x-7x per week dropdown). Use existing Drawer component as wrapper.
3. Ensure TypeScript types are correct and pnpm build passes. The component should handle loading state and empty state gracefully.
4. Verify: cd /projects/sandbox/ChronAI/frontend && pnpm build passes without errors
acceptance_criteria: pnpm build passes with the new HabitsDrawer component
HabitsDrawer fetches real habits from /api/habits (no hardcoded data)
Each habit shows streak with flame icon, 7-day dots, and SVG streak ring
Check-in button calls API and shows framer-motion animation
Add-habit form allows creating new habits with name + frequency
API helpers (fetchHabits, checkinHabit, createHabit, deleteHabit, fetchWeeklyReview) exist in lib/api.ts
findings: Updated dashboard/page.tsx to pass accessToken prop to HabitsDrawer. Build passes cleanly. Used same patterns as TasksDrawer/CalendarDrawer for data fetching.
